---
title: "deseq2_demo"
author: "Sreeram Peela"
date: "2026-03-04"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Data downloads

Data can be downloaded directly from NCBI GEO (via FTP/HTTP) or through dedicated R packages like GEOquery, cBioDataPack, or TCGABiolinks

```{r}
install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("cBioPortalData")
BiocManager::install("TCGAbiolinks")
BiocManager::install("GEOquery")
```

#### Demonstrating cBioDataPack

```{r}
library(cBioPortalData)
cbio <- cBioPortal() # setting up an instance
studies <- getStudies(cbio, buildReport = TRUE)
head(studies)
```

The dataset can be downloaded entirely using cBioDataPack or partially using the cBioPortalData functions

```{r}
laml <- cBioDataPack(cancer_study_id = "laml_tcga",
  use_cache = TRUE,
  names.field = c("Hugo_Symbol", "Entrez_Gene_Id", "Gene"),
  cleanup = TRUE, ask = FALSE)
laml # entire data types - multiomics
```

Each of the experiment class can be accessed as a named-list object

```{r}
laml_rsem_rnaseq <- laml[['mrna_seq_v2_rsem']]
laml_rsem_rnaseq
```

Each object is a SummarisedExperiment class. Refer to the figure below: ![](./deseq_demo/summarisedExperiment.png)

Load this library to work with the datasets

```{r}
library(SummarizedExperiment)
```

```{r}
genes_of_interest <- rownames(laml_rsem_rnaseq)[1:50]
samples_of_interest <- c("TCGA-AB-2803-03", "TCGA-AB-2805-03", "TCGA-AB-2806-03", "TCGA-AB-2807-03", "TCGA-AB-2808-03")
```

```{r}
laml_subset <- cBioPortalData(api = cbio, studyId = "laml_tcga", ask = FALSE,
                       by = "hugoGeneSymbol",
                       genes = genes_of_interest,molecularProfileIds =  c('laml_tcga_rna_seq_v2_mrna')
                       )
laml_subset # selected data layer and subset of genes present
```

We can now subset according to the sample ids

```{r}
valid_samples <- intersect(
  samples_of_interest,
  colnames(laml_subset[[1]])
)
counts_subset <- laml_subset[[1]][, valid_samples]

actual_counts <- assay(counts_subset)
```

We can now construct a deg class based on our requirement by extracting the counts and metadata.

#### GEOquery demo

```{r}
library(GEOquery)
```

```{r}
options(download.file.method.GEOquery = "libcurl")

gse <- getGEO( GEO = 'GSE5364', # microarray dataset
               filename = NULL, 
               destdir = getwd(), # write in the current dir
               GSElimits = NULL, 
               GSEMatrix = TRUE,# get the matrix
               AnnotGPL = FALSE, 
               getGPL = TRUE, 
               parseCharacteristics = TRUE )
```

```{r}
length(gse)
```

```{r}
gse[[1]]
```

As the above is an expressionset, we can extract the counts using exprs function.

```{r}
counts_matrix <- exprs(gse[[1]])
```
